LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MCLARENS YOUNG INTERNATIONAL INC, CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S OF LONDON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE INSURANCE POLICY PROVIDED FOR A TWO-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (UF #13.) PLAINTIFFS' CAUSES OF ACTIONS ACCRUED ON MAY 20, 2008, WHEN THE LAST CHECK OMITTING THEIR NAMES WAS ISSUED. PLAINTIFFS FILED THE COMPLAINT ON DECEMBER 27, 2011, MORE THAN TWO YEARS AFTER THEIR CAUSES OF ACTIONS ACCRUED. PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS AGAINST MCLARENS YOUNG FAIL BECAUSE THAT DEFENDANT OWED NO CONTRACTUAL OR COMMON LAW DUTY TO PLAINTIFFS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

TRIAL MOTION IN DEPT. 504 HELD. NOTICE FOR MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO CONFORM TO PROOF DENIED. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: R. GUMPAL, REPORTER: DEBBIE ACEVEDO-RAMIREZ, CSR#7692, ATKINSON-BAKER DEPO.COM, TEL. (415) 421-3021, (504)
ORDER ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF SUMMARY OF JUDGMENT AND FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO CONFORM TO PROOF

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFFS RICHARD RESMA AND CYNTHIA RESMA'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO CONFORM TO PROOF - PLAINTIFFS' MOTIONS TO RECONSIDER AND TO AMEND ARE DENIED. THE COURT, HAVING ALREADY ENTERED JUDGMENT, CAN NO LONGER ENTERTAIN THESE MOTIONS. THE COURT DECLINES PLAINTIFFS' INVITATION IN THEIR REPLY BRIEF TO TREAT THESE MOTIONS AS REQUESTS TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT UNDER CCP SECTION 473(B). SUCH A REQUEST IS BOTH PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER AND SUBSTANTIVELY UNSUPPORTED. EVEN IF THE COURT COULD CONSIDER THE MOTION TO RECONSIDER, IT WOULD DENY IT. PLAINTIFFS HAD THE ALLEGEDLY NEW INFORMATION BEFORE FILING THEIR OPPOSITION AND WELL BEFORE HEARING ON THE MOTION. WERE IT ESSENTIAL TO THEIR OPPOSITION, THEY NEEDED TO BRING IT TO THE COURT'S ATTENTION THEN. IN ANY EVENT, THE ALLEGEDLY NEW INFORMATION WOULD NOT CHANGE THE OUTCOME. IT DOES NOT SUPPORT A VIABLE BASIS FOR TOLLING THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS THAT BEGAN RUNNING ONCE THE CHECKS WERE ISSUED. IF A TIMELY NOTICE TO APPEAR TO CONTEST THIS TENTATIVE RULING IS GIVEN, THE HEARING WILL TAKE PLACE IN DEPARTMENT 504 BEFORE JUDGE PETER J. BUSCH AT 9:00 A.M. ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 25, 2013. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302)

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn For Reconsideration Of Summary Judgment And For Leave To Amend Complaint To Conform To Proof
Off Calendar Nov-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFFs RICHARD RESMA and CYNTHIA RESMA's motion For Reconsideration Of Summary Judgment And For Leave To Amend Complaint To Conform To Proof - Plaintiffs' motions to reconsider and to amend are denied. The Court, having already entered judgment, can no longer entertain these motions. The Court declines Plaintiffs' invitation in their reply brief to treat these motions as requests to vacate the judgment under CCP section 473(b). Such a request is both procedurally improper and substantively unsupported. Even if the Court could consider the motion to reconsider, it would deny it. Plaintiffs had the allegedly new information before filing their opposition and well before hearing on the motion. Were it essential to their opposition, they needed to bring it to the Court's attention then. In any event, the allegedly new information would not change the outcome. It does not support a viable basis for tolling the statute of limitations that began running once the checks were issued.
If a timely notice to appear to contest this tentative ruling is given, the hearing will take place in Department 504 before Judge Peter J. Busch at 9:00 a.m. on Monday, November 25, 2013. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/ehg

Notice For Motion For Reconsideration For Summary Judgment And For Leave To Amend Complaint To Conform To Proof
Off Calendar Nov-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward D. Winchester, Esq. (415) 3523-2713 for Defendant Underwriters of London et al; Sidney Hall, Esq. (650) 342-1830 for Plaintiff Richard S. Resma; no other appearances.

Both parties submit final arguments for Notice for Motion for Reconsideration for Summary Judgment and For Leave to Amend Complaint to Conform to Proof. RULING: After arguments are submitted, the Court now adopts its tentative ruling as its final ruling. The Court has already entered the judgment and can no longer entertain these motions. The Court declines Plaintiff's requests to vacate the judgment under CCP section 473 (b). Plaintiff's motions to reconsider and for leave to amend are DENIED. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Rosallie Gumpal, Reporter: Debbie Acevedo-Ramirez, CSR#7692, Atkinson-Baker Depo.Com, Tel. (415) 421-3021, (504)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-07-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SAMUEL RUBY, ESQ., AND EDWARD WINCHESTER, ESQ., 415.652.2700, FOR DEFENDANTS MCLARENS YOUNG INTERNATIONAL INC. & CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S OF LONDON; No other appearances.

RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MCLARENS YOUNG INTERNATIONAL INC, CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S OF LONDON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The insurance policy provided for a two-year statute of limitations. (UF #13.) Plaintiffs' causes of actions accrued on May 20, 2008, when the last check omitting their names was issued. Plaintiffs filed the complaint on December 27, 2011, more than two years after their causes of actions accrued. Plaintiffs' claims against McLarens Young fail because that defendant owed no contractual or common law duty to Plaintiffs. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S OF LONDON subscribing to Policy No. LAR000367'S Motion to Compel the Deposition Appearance of Cecilia De Leon is GRANTED. Cecilia De Leon is ORDERED to comply with the Deposition Subpoena issued on 8-1-13 and personally served on 8-2-13 and appear for deposition on 9-23-13 at 9:00 a.m. at 601 California Street, Suite 1800, San Francisco, CA 94108 and to bring with her those documents in her possession, custody or control which are responsive to Attachment 3 to the Subpoena. A deponent who disobeys a deposition subpoena may be punished for contempt and forfeit $500 to the deposing party. (CCP 2020.240) In addition: Subject to any other arrangements made with Mr. Winchester. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Peter J. Van Zandt; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
